results.possibleIdentity: Joel D. Ash (1936–2020) was a prominent electrical engineer and corporate executive who had a long career at International Telephone and Telegraph (ITT), serving as an executive with extensive international responsibilities. He was an alumnus of Dartmouth College (Class of 1956) and the Thayer School of Engineering, where he earned a Master of Science in Electrical Engineering in 1958. His technical expertise focused on telecommunications, specifically in areas such as premises wiring, data communications, and machine communication; he co-authored the technical book 'Premises Wiring for the Information Age' (1994). Beyond his engineering career, Ash was deeply involved in the arts as a poet and limerick author, publishing 'Potpourri of the Mind' (1997) and 'A Sojourn in Limerick' (1999). This combination of technical leadership and artistic output aligns with the profile of engineers associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) movement, which sought to foster collaborations between the technical and creative communities. Throughout his career, he was based in the New York City area, including residences in Connecticut and links to Massapequa, New York, before retiring to Grantham, New Hampshire, in 2002.
